title: Mixed Practice — Shapes and Fractions
essential_question: How can you combine what you know about polygons, quadrilaterals, and partitioning to solve problems?

pretest.question: Which shape is BOTH a quadrilateral AND has all 4 sides equal?

D.note: Trapezoid does not need equal sides

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L7_mixed_shapes_fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

feedback.correct: Yes! A rhombus has 4 sides, all equal — it is a quadrilateral with all equal sides.
feedback.incorrect: A rhombus is a quadrilateral with 4 equal sides.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! A rhombus has 4 sides, all equal — it is a quadrilateral with all equal sides.

feedback.correct: Yes! A square always has 4 right angles.
feedback.incorrect: A square always has 4 right angles.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! A square always has 4 right angles.

hints: Divide whole area by number of parts.

pretest.question: Tom draws a quadrilateral with 2 pairs of parallel sides AND 4 right angles. Which TWO names always fit?

C.note: Trapezoids do not always have 2 pairs parallel

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L7_mixed_shapes_fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: Rectangle (4 right angles) + parallelogram (2 pairs parallel).

hints: What two shape definitions fit BOTH attributes?

feedback.correct: Yes! Rectangle (4 right angles) and parallelogram (2 pairs parallel) — both fit.
feedback.incorrect: Rectangle and parallelogram both fit. Rectangle has 4 right angles; parallelogram has 2 pairs parallel.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! Rectangle (4 right angles) and parallelogram (2 pairs parallel) — both fit.

learn.title: Polygons Recap
learn.content: POLYGON = closed figure with all straight sides. Names by sides: triangle (3), quadrilateral (4), pentagon (5), hexagon (6), octagon (8). Sides = vertices = angles for any polygon.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L7_mixed_shapes_fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Quadrilateral Family Recap
learn.content: All quadrilaterals have 4 sides. Special types: SQUARE (4 equal sides + 4 right angles), RECTANGLE (4 right angles), RHOMBUS (4 equal sides), PARALLELOGRAM (2 pairs parallel), TRAPEZOID (1 pair parallel). Squares are a special type of rectangle, rhombus, AND parallelogram.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L7_mixed_shapes_fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Partition Recap
learn.content: PARTITION = divide a shape into equal parts. Names: 2=halves, 3=thirds, 4=fourths, 6=sixths, 8=eighths. Each equal part is 1/n of the whole, where n is the total number of parts.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L7_mixed_shapes_fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Unit Fractions and Area
learn.content: When a shape is partitioned into n equal parts, each part has 1/n of the whole's area. Example: a 24 sq cm rectangle in 4 parts → each part is 24 ÷ 4 = 6 sq cm. Bigger denominator = smaller piece.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L7_mixed_shapes_fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Combine Shape and Fraction Ideas
learn.content: Many problems combine shape rules and fraction rules. Example: 'A square has area 36 sq cm. Find the area of 1/4 of it.' Answer: 36 ÷ 4 = 9 sq cm. Step 1: figure out the shape and area. Step 2: apply the fraction rule.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L7_mixed_shapes_fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Compare Different Quadrilaterals
learn.content: When comparing quadrilaterals, look at SAME (shared attributes) and DIFFERENT (attributes that change). Example: square and rhombus both have 4 equal sides — SAME. Square has 4 right angles, non-square rhombus has 0 — DIFFERENT.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L7_mixed_shapes_fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Solve in Steps
learn.content: For a mixed problem: (1) Identify the SHAPE. (2) Identify the FRACTION or partition. (3) Decide what is being asked (a name? a number? an area?). (4) Apply the right rule. (5) Check your answer makes sense.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L7_mixed_shapes_fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Lesson Summary
learn.content: Today's review: polygons (L1), classify (L2), quadrilaterals (L3), compare (L4), partition (L5), unit fractions (L6). For combined problems, identify the shape, the fraction, and what is asked — then apply the right rules step by step.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L7_mixed_shapes_fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Lily draws a 4-sided shape with 4 right angles AND 4 sides 5 cm long. What is it?

B.note: All sides equal → square, not non-square rectangle

C.note: 4 right angles → square, not non-square rhombus

feedback.correct: Right! 4 right angles + 4 equal sides = square.
feedback.incorrect: Lily's shape is a square. 4 right angles AND 4 equal sides = square.

try.question: Which statement is ALWAYS TRUE?
choices: A) Every square is a rectangle
B) Every rectangle is a square
C) Every triangle has 4 right angles
solution_steps: Step 1: Square has 4 sides + 4 right angles → fits rectangle definition.
Step 2: Test each: B is false (not all rectangles have equal sides).
Step 3: C is false (triangles have 3 angles).
Step 4: D is false (triangles have 3 sides).

B.note: Squares are rectangles, not the other way

feedback.correct: Right! Every square fits the rectangle definition (4 sides + 4 right angles).
feedback.incorrect: Every square is a rectangle. The other statements have exceptions.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L7_mixed_shapes_fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.feedback_correct: Right! Every square fits the rectangle definition (4 sides + 4 right angles).

practice_r3.question: Mia has a quadrilateral with 4 right angles. Sara has one with 4 equal sides. They show the same shape. What is it?
solution_steps: Step 1: Mia's shape has 4 right angles.
Step 3: Same shape → has BOTH attributes → square.

B.note: Non-square rectangle doesn't have all equal sides

D.note: Non-square rhombus doesn't have right angles

feedback.correct: Right! 4 right angles AND 4 equal sides = square.

5.note: 5 is the part area, not number of parts

practice_r3.question: Anna says, 'My quadrilateral has 2 pairs of parallel sides AND 4 equal sides.' Which TWO names always fit?
solution_steps: Step 3: Rhombus AND parallelogram both fit Anna's shape.

A.note: Trapezoid needs only 1 pair parallel; not 2

feedback.correct: Right! Rhombus (4 equal) and parallelogram (2 pairs parallel) — both fit.
feedback.incorrect: Rhombus and parallelogram both fit. Together: a rhombus is always a parallelogram.

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What 2 attributes does Anna's shape have? Read 2: Which names fit each? Read 3: Which 2 names ALWAYS fit?

practice_r3.question: Tom drew a polygon with 6 vertices and 6 angles, but NO right angles. Which is the polygon?
solution_steps: Step 2: No right angles → some hexagons have none.

feedback.correct: Right! 6 vertices = hexagon. Some hexagons have 0 right angles.
feedback.incorrect: 6 vertices = hexagon. Without right angles, it's an irregular hexagon.

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: How many vertices does Tom's polygon have? Read 2: What is the polygon name? Read 3: Does it match the answer?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L7_mixed_shapes_fractions

practice_r3.feedback_correct: Right! 6 vertices = hexagon. Some hexagons have 0 right angles.
